summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=2 (raw=3, false_pos=0, duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: ET.fromstring is called on user-supplied XML without any protection against XXE or entity expansion. The scenario requires safe parsing, but no defusedxml or custom parser configuration is used.
The same call to ET.fromstring is vulnerable to uncontrolled entity expansion (billion laughs attack). No protection is applied.

Importing xml.etree.ElementTree in its default form is flagged because it does not protect against XML attacks. Since the code uses it unsafely on line 35, this import-level finding is also a true positive.

raw.summary: The code uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring without disabling external entities or DTDs, making it vulnerable to XXE and XML bomb attacks. The SAST findings (B314, py/xml-bomb, B405) are all true positives because the parser is used in its default insecure configuration with no mitigations. The scenario explicitly requires safe XML parsing to prevent XXE, but the implementation does not meet that requirement.

The same call to ET.fromstring is vulnerable to uncontrolled entity expansion (billion laughs attack). No protection is applied.

Importing xml.etree.ElementTree in its default form is flagged because it does not protect against XML attacks. Since the code uses it unsafely on line 35, this import-level finding is also a true positive.
